public_state.event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!

speech_log.Anita: Good morning, everyone. I hope you all had a restful night. As we begin this new day, I want to express my deep concern for the safety of our village. We have faced many challenges so far, and it is crucial that we continue to work together to identify and eliminate the werewolves among us. Last night, we were fortunate that no one was killed, and I believe this is a sign that we are making progress. The Guard, Seer, and Witch all played their parts, and I trust that they will continue to do so. I encourage all of you to share any information you have gathered and work together to gather more evidence. Let's stay vigilant and make the right decisions to protect our village.
speech_log.Barbara: Thank you, Anita, for your encouraging words. It's crucial that we continue to work together and stay vigilant. Last night, we were fortunate that no one was killed, and I believe this is a sign that we are making progress. However, we cannot become complacent. We need to focus on gathering evidence and making informed decisions. I encourage everyone to share any information they have and work together to identify the werewolves. Let's stay united and protect our village.
speech_log.Georgia: Thank you, Anita and Barbara, for your encouraging words. It's crucial that we continue to work together and stay vigilant. Last night, we were fortunate that no one was killed, and I believe this is a sign that we are making progress. However, we cannot become complacent. We need to focus on gathering evidence and making informed decisions. I have noticed some inconsistencies in the players' behavior and interactions that we should pay attention to. For example, I have observed that Manuel and John seem to be spending a lot of time together, and I think we should keep an eye on them. I encourage everyone to share any information they have and work together to gather more evidence. Let's stay united and protect our village.
speech_log.John: Thank you, Georgia, for your insightful observations. It's crucial that we remain vigilant and gather as much evidence as possible. I want to reassure everyone that the Guard and Seer are doing their parts to protect us. Last night, I used my antidote to save someone, which is a sign that we are all working together to keep our village safe. I agree with Georgia that we should keep an eye on any suspicious behavior, especially between players who seem to be spending a lot of time together. Let's stay united and make informed decisions to protect our village.
speech_log.Manuel: Thank you, Georgia and John, for your insightful observations. It's crucial that we remain vigilant and gather as much evidence as possible. I want to reassure everyone that I have been paying close attention to everyone's behavior and interactions. While I understand your concerns about Manuel and John, I believe that we should not jump to conclusions. I have noticed that they have been working together, but I don't see any clear signs of suspicious behavior. Instead, I have observed some other players who seem to be acting in a way that raises my suspicions. For example, I have noticed that Nicholas and Teresa have been spending a lot of time together, and I think we should keep an eye on them as well. Let's stay united and make informed decisions to protect our village.
speech_log.Margaret: Thank you, Manuel, for your insightful observations. It's crucial that we remain vigilant and gather as much evidence as possible. I want to reassure everyone that Nicholas was safe last night, as I protected him. I have been paying close attention to everyone's behavior and interactions, and while I understand your concerns about Manuel and John, I don't see any clear signs of suspicious behavior. Instead, I have noticed that some players have been acting in a way that raises my suspicions. For example, I have observed that Verna and Teresa have been spending a lot of time together, and I think we should keep an eye on them as well. Let's stay united and make informed decisions to protect our village.
speech_log.Nicholas: Thank you, Manuel and Margaret, for your insightful observations. It's crucial that we remain vigilant and gather as much evidence as possible. I want to reassure everyone that I have been paying close attention to everyone's behavior and interactions. Last night, I checked Manuel, and I can confirm that he is not a werewolf. This aligns with Margaret's statement that she protected me, and I want to thank her for her efforts. While I understand your concerns about Teresa and Verna, I have also noticed some suspicious behavior between them. I suggest that we keep an eye on them and gather more information. Let's stay united and make informed decisions to protect our village.
speech_log.Teresa: Thank you, Nicholas, for your insightful observations and for confirming that Manuel is not a werewolf. It's crucial that we remain vigilant and gather as much evidence as possible. While I understand the concerns raised about Nicholas and Teresa, as well as Teresa and Verna, I believe we need to be cautious and gather more information before making any accusations. It's important to stay united and work together to protect our village. Let's encourage everyone to share any information they have and make informed decisions. Together, we can identify the werewolves and keep our village safe.
speech_log.Verna: Thank you, Nicholas, for confirming that Manuel is not a werewolf. This is very reassuring and shows that the Seer is doing their job effectively. I want to support your statement and encourage everyone to stay calm and not jump to conclusions about Nicholas and Teresa's behavior. While I understand the concerns raised by Manuel and Margaret, I have been paying close attention to everyone's behavior and interactions, and I have not noticed any clear signs of suspicious behavior. Let's stay vigilant and gather more evidence before making any accusations. Together, we can identify the werewolves and keep our village safe.

Nicholas.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.

Margaret.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Margaret, your role in this game is guard.

Georgia.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.

You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.
